title: The LORD's love and the priests' polluted offerings
summary_text: The LORD declares his love for Jacob while exposing Edom's downfall as proof that his covenant choice stands. He then rebukes the priests for offering polluted bread and dishonoring his name at the altar. The chapter contrasts the LORD's great name among the nations with careless worship in Judah.
key_points: The LORD reaffirms his covenant love for Jacob.
Edom is shown as ruined and rejected.
The priests despise the altar with polluted offerings.
God's name will be great among the nations.
evidence_phrases: my name will be great among the nations

title: The covenant with Levi and treachery in marriage
summary_text: Malachi warns the priests that the covenant with Levi was meant to produce reverence, truth, and upright instruction, not stumbling. He also confronts men who have dealt treacherously with the wife of their youth and covered the altar with tears. The chapter ties broken marriage faithfulness to broken worship.
key_points: The priestly covenant is recalled and judged.
Levi's intended role is instruction in truth.
Men are rebuked for treachery in marriage.
God rejects hypocritical worship tied to betrayal.
evidence_phrases: covering the altar of the LORD

title: The messenger, the refiner, and the tithe
summary_text: The LORD promises to send a messenger who will prepare the way, and then he will come as a refiner to purify Levi and judge sorcery, oppression, and deceit. The people are also called to return in tithes and offerings, and God responds by opening the windows of heaven. The chapter closes with a book of remembrance for those who fear the LORD.
key_points: A messenger prepares the LORD's way.
The LORD comes to refine and judge.
The people are told to bring the whole tithe.
Those who fear God are remembered before him.

title: The day of the LORD and Elijah's coming
summary_text: Malachi ends with the coming day that will burn like a furnace for the proud but heal with the sun of righteousness for those who fear God's name. The people are told to remember the law of Moses and to expect Elijah before the great and terrible day arrives. The book closes by joining warning, memory, and hope.
key_points: The day of the LORD will burn the arrogant.
The sun of righteousness will rise for the faithful.
Israel must remember the law of Moses.
Elijah will come before the great day.
evidence_phrases: turn the heart of the fathers
